entry.title: SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA now supports SAP S/4HANA private Cloud / RISE Target Versions
entry.content:
Dear All,

SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA can now be used for both SAP S/4HANA (on premise) and SAP S/4HANA private Cloud (PCE/RISE) target product versions.

​SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA private Cloud are developed in the same codeline and are identical concerning the relevant simplification items. Nevertheless, there are differences in the compatibility scope items and successor functionalities (for example the successor of FI-TV on premise is Travel Management, on PCE the successor is SAP Concur).

The correct target SAP S/4HANA version is selected in the dropdown list in the SAP Readiness Check data collection report RC_COLLECT_ANALYSIS_DATA:

Please note: To see the SAP S/4HANA private Cloud entries in the list, you may need to update your local replica of the simplification item catalog.

To determine whether any action is required, go to transaction SA38 in the system to be analyzed and run report RC_COLLECT_ANALYSIS_DATA. If the SAP S/4HANA private Cloud versions are not yet visible in the Target S/4HANA Version list, the local replica of the simplification item catalog needs to be updated.

To update the Simplification Item Catalog, perform the following steps:

    1. Execute the report /SDF/RC_START_CHECK in the source system via transaction SA38.
    2. Choose the Update Catalog with latest version from SAP button.

 As a result, the local replica of the simplification item catalog will be updated. You can now run report RC_COLLECT_ANALYSIS_DATA again, and the Target S/4HANA Version list will now include the latest generally available SAP S/4HANA product versions.

----------------

Manual Upload of the Simplification Item Catalog

Alternatively, if the SAP-SUPPORT_PORTAL RFC destination is unable to connect with the SAP support backbone, you can update the simplification item catalog content manually:

    1. Open the simplification item catalog content in SAP for Me
    2. Choose the Download Simplification Item icon to download the content to your local computer.
    3. Run the report /SDF/RC_START_CHECK.
    4. Choose the Upload Simplification Item Catalog from the file button and upload the content from where you saved it in step 2.

Now you can execute report RC_COLLECT_ANALYSIS_DATA again. The latest SAP S/4HANA product versions will now be visible in the Target S/4HANA Version list. Proceed as described in SAP Note 2913617 (SAP S/4HANA conversions) or SAP Note 3059197 (SAP S/4HANA upgrades) to perform the data collection and create the new analysis.

entry.title: SAP Certification for Partners: An Overview of the Latest Changes - Including Q&A
entry.content:
On March 19, Suzanne Heiob, SAP Certification Product Management, and Axel Meinke, Partner Enablement Manager MEE, invited SAP partners to provide information on the latest updates in the SAP certification program. The German session offered insights into the transformation of the certification program and introduced a range of updates. It also provided space for questions and discussion. To help you as partners prepare for the new requirements, here is a focused summary of the webinar content and related questions from the Q&A.

 

What's New in the SAP Certification Program?

A key change in the certification program is the focus on role-based certifications. The goal is to align the skills of SAP partners precisely with practical requirements. The new certifications cover critical roles such as Enterprise Architects and Implementation Consultants, which were not sufficiently represented in the past due to the strong product focus. In addition, certifications that are no longer relevant have been removed from the portfolio to focus on supporting cloud deployments and S/4HANA transformations.

 

Additional Certification Requirements for SAP Partners

By July 2025, all partner resources in key roles – enterprise architects, developers, business transformation management consultants, project managers involved in RISE with SAP projects – must have a valid SAP certification. By the end of 2025, it is expected that 50% of relevant partner practitioners will hold at least one valid certification in the SAP solution portfolio. Overall, this means that SAP partners will need to continuously update and expand their skills.

 

The 'Get- and Stay-Certified' Model

Starting April 2024, certifications will need to be renewed annually through a ‘Stay-Certified’ assessment. This short quiz generally requires up to two hours of learning effort, lasts 20–30 minutes, and extends the certification by 12 months. This is particularly important for those who wish to retain their certifications after March 31, 2025, as without taking such an assessment, the certification status will expire.

Continuous proof of knowledge ensures that experts are always up to date with SAP innovations. This secures both the quality of SAP projects and individual competitiveness in the job market. To make this visible, there are digital badges available for both completing certifications and completing Learning Journeys (including a short self-test). These badges can be shared on LinkedIn or viewed on Credly.

 

SAP Universal ID: Simplified Certification Transfer

An important innovation is the introduction of the SAP Universal ID. This ID is a lifelong personal identifier that facilitates the management of certifications across different employers. At least two different email addresses, including a private one, must be linked to activate the Universal ID. This ensures that access to the ID remains even when switching companies.

 

SAP Learning Hub and Certification

A subscription to SAP Learning Hub includes access to all SAP learning materials and four certification attempts per year. The costs for the initial certification and the 'Stay Certified' assessments are included in the SAP Learning Hub subscription. A certification remains valid for 12 months as long as regular assessments are passed. Re-certification does not require a certification voucher and can be attempted an unlimited number of times.

Live sessions in SAP Learning Hub are particularly helpful for exam preparation, such as the 'Structured Guided Learning' or 'Stay Certified' formats. Furthermore, you gain access to pre-configured training systems for practical exercises. With a subscription to SAP Learning Hub, you are well equipped: You have the flexibility to design your learning times individually, prepare for exams, and take them. This combination of independent learning, practical exercise opportunities in test environments, and interactive sessions ensures that you are optimally prepared for your certifications.

The Learning Hub Adoption Dashboard is also a valuable tool that allows learning managers to monitor and manage the certification transformation within the company. Here you can view relevant information about the learning and certification status of your employees and coordinate necessary steps to secure certification status before the deadline.

 

Q&A from the Session (originially in German, translated below)

Universal ID

 

I have a question about the Universal ID. Why do I need to link two email addresses? And why must the two email addresses have different domains? This results in needing to link a private email address for everyone requiring a Universal SAP ID.

The purpose of the second email is for recovery, in case you lose your access credentials. It's recommended to use a private email as a recovery option because the UID accompanies you throughout your career (unlike the old user model where S-Users, for example, belonged to the employer).

If you're experiencing the error: "Can't see your certifications? Your emails linked to SAP Universal ID are not verified.” How can you verify your email if it has been the associated email for years?

 

You should have received a verification email to the corresponding address. If it's not there, please check your spam folder.

 

Certification

 

Where can I find an overview of which certifications already exist in my company?

If you're a Learning Manager, you can request the reporter role for the Learning Hub Dashboard by submitting a ticket. You'll find an overview of your certified consultants under Certifications (provided the learners have a license for SAP Learning Hub).

I have certifications under different S-User/C-User accounts. What do I need to do to transfer these certifications to the new UID?

You need to generate the UID and add the certified S-/P-Users (simple context menu – "Add User" in the process).

Do certifications obtained without LH not count for the company?

They do count – you can also directly purchase certification attempts. Re-certification is only possible via SAP LH.

Is there an overview of certifications available without Learning Hub access?

You can find an overview of all certifications here: https://learning.sap.com/certifications?page=1. The overview of your certified learners, regardless of SAP Learning Hub licenses, can also be found in SAP for Me.

Can a certification completed in 2022 on an old Universal ID (with a previous employer) be transferred to a new one, and if so, how?The UID does not belong to an employer like the user in the old model but is personal to you. In your case, you simply need to add the S-User ID of your new employer to your existing UID.

It has recently been communicated that special certification requirements are being imposed on SAP partners. What (type) of certifications are specifically needed? Are there must-have certifications?

For partners, certifications listed in the Partner Competency Framework are particularly relevant: https://partneredge.sap.com/en/partnership/manage/competency/requirements.html

What does the status "certified stay" “completed” and “in progress” mean?

The status "completed" does not exist. We have on SAP Learning the status "up to date" when someone is valid with their current certification and the due date is a long time away. Then there is "Stay certified due", when the due date is within the next two weeks.

Currently, it's still possible to purchase certification attempts separately without LH. At the end of last year, SAP communicated frequently that a license for SAP Learning Hub will soon be mandatory and that certifications cannot be acquired mid-term without Learning Hub. Is there a clear, definitive statement from SAP on this matter? Can certification attempts still be purchased separately without a license for SAP Learning Hub?

"Stay certified": this requires a valid SAP Learning Hub subscription.

 

"Stay Certified": this requires a valid SAP Learning Hub subscription.

 

There is a separate package with 2 standalone certification attempts. These attempts can only be used for the initial certification. Delays for extending validity must subsequently be completed through LH; therefore, LH is necessary long-term to remain certified.

Is there an overview listing which certifications bring how many points towards partner status? How do we know which certifications will be retired and which will remain?

The Partner Competency Framework can be found here: https://partneredge.sap.com/en/partnership/manage/competency/requirements.html

 

The list of all certifications can be found here: https://learning.sap.com/certifications?page=1. Any certifications not listed there have already been retired.

If the exam for re-certification is not completed by March 31, 2025, can the exam still be taken later?

Yes, but you lose your status and are no longer certified until you pass the new exam. Instead of simply taking the shorter re-certification test, you need to get fully certified again.

The News Alert states "By July this year, all partner resources in key roles – such as enterprise architects, developers, business transformations management consultants, and project managers involved in RISE with SAP projects – must have a valid SAP certification." Which certifications are specifically meant here?

You can find an overview of the necessary training and certifications for GROW and RISE in the following link: https://partneredge.sap.com/en/partnership/manage/competency/requirements.html

 

Is the competency relevance of a certificate visible only in the Learning Hub Adoption Dashboard or is this overview available outside of this dashboard?

The Partner Competency Framework can also be found here: https://partneredge.sap.com/en/partnership/manage/competency.html

Can we use certifications from our external consultants? Even if they are not employed by us?

 

Once the external consultant receives an S-User from your company and links this S-User to their SAP Universal ID, this certification will also be counted for your company.

How is the certification status of consultants in projects verified?

Via the Digital Badge, which indicates whether a consultant is still validly certified.

What does the status "Completed" mean in SAP for me?

Someone has completed the stay certified assessment and extended their certification.

What does "Stay certified" = "In Progress" mean?

This means that the learner has created a Universal ID but has yet to pass an assessment.

Are there new or additional certifications for authorization consultants planned?

Currently, no new certification is planned. Here is the portal to check if there is a successor for a specific certification: https://www.sap.com/certificationfinder

Learning Manager

 

How do I become a Learning Manager or nominate others?

Initially in the contract – for further nominations, information can be found here: https://partneredge.sap.com/en/partnership/manage/training/certified.html

What resources are available for Learning Managers?

The Learning Manager Hub (https://partneredge.sap.com/en/partnership/manage/training/certified.html)in the Partner Portal provides Q&A, email templates, and other helpful resources.

SAP Learning Hub

 

When I want to log in to the Learning Hub via web, I receive the message "HTTP Status 400 – Bad Request." Is there a new link for this? (https://learninghub.sap.com/login)

https://learninghub.sap.com is the official page for the SAP Learning Hub. Issues might also be browser-related. Try using a different browser in case of problems.

I want to know how I can obtain the "Learning Manager" permission.

The "IT Contact Person" automatically gains access to the Learning Analytics Dashboard for all learners. When purchasing, you can request admin authorization for additional users via ticket.

Learning.sap.com

 

Why are there now two pages: Learning.sap.com and Learninghub.sap.com? Will they be merged in the future?

That is the long-term plan. Unfortunately, in the ongoing operation, this can only be gradually adjusted, including the information from training.sap.com that is also being migrated. The goal is to have everything on learning.sap.com. For example, openSAP courses, certification information, and more have already been migrated there. It is increasingly becoming the single point of access, where some content or features, such as re-certification or hands-on systems, live sessions, are only accessible through the Learning Hub subscription.

 

Additional Resources

You can find more information and details on the topic at the following addresses – feel free to browse:

  • German materials from the session can be found here: Recording & Slides
  • SAP Learning Hub – Overview of the Learning Platform
  • Overview of all SAP certifications
  • SAP Certification Finder: Find the right certification for your needs
  • SAP Certification on learning.sap.com: Your central entry point
  • Partner Competency Framework: All requirements for SAP partners
  • Stay updated on training and certification with the SAP Learning Newsletter (German version here)!

Do you have questions? Feel free to leave them in the comments!

entry.title: Step-by-Step guide: Adding Image Captions in SAP Community
entry.content:
Below is a step-by-step guide on how to add captions to images in the SAP Community platform. The example shows the blog post creation interface, but it’s relevant for all content creation scenarios in SAP Community. 

  1. Accessing content creation interface 
  • Go to community.sap.com  
  • Click on a category (for example CRM and Customer experience)
  • Click on “Create a Post”:  
 

Showing the position of a Create a Post button in a community category page

-Click on “Choose a board”:  

 

Showing in the blog creation UI where to click in order to choose a board

  -Click on “Show all”:  

 Showing in the blog post creation UI how to view all boards you can select from

 

-Select the board where you would like to post your content:   

 Showing how to select the board in which you want to create your blog post

 

2. Positioning your image 

- Position your cursor where you want the image to appear 

- From the formatting toolbar at the top of the editor, select the Camera icon (labeled “Insert Photos” in the tooltip) 

Showing where the camera icon is in the blog post creation UI

- This will open the choose file dialog window  

 3. Selecting image file  

-Click on “Choose Files”:  

 

Shows where to click in order to Choose files 

  • Select the image you want to use and click “Open”:  

 Shows how to select an image and clicking on "Open" to have used in the post  

 4. 

Adding caption: 

After uploading, you'll see a field for entering the caption. 

  • - Now Put the cursor in white space below the image 
  • Enter the text according to the best practices shared above 
  • Follow the best practices discussed earlier. 
  • Click “Done”  
 

Showing where you should add the alternative text

  •  Click “Post”:  

Shows where to click in order to post the blog post

 Once published, this is how your image will appear with the caption below it:  

 

Showing the published blog post and the position of the alternative text

entry.title: Re: CLT: Copy country by itself
entry.content:
Dear Derek,

Using CLT, it is only allowed to create local versions for country/region that is not delivered by SAP in standard. 

For the supported country/region in CLT refer to SAP Note: 3107866

Regarding the changing the local currency it is not possible in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. If you like to have this feature in public cloud, I suggest you to raise it as customer influence request in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Finance campaign.

Regards

Sathya Nallusamy

entry.title: Re: Downloading a Report Output in SAP Without Using Spool Request
entry.content:
The obligatory "thanks for sharing" pat on the back but if I'm completely honest, this is just not a good post for many reasons.

Let's ignore the fact that this is "ABAP like it's 1999" and focus on the premise. The post starts with "download file in a readable format (e.g., .txt, .csv, .xlsx)". One might argue that every format is readable if you try hard enough. But when it comes to the file operations, in ABAP we usually distinguish between the plain text and binary format. Plain text would be TXT, CSV and the likes. Binary format would be anything else, most typically PDF or Excel format.

In the code example, the data is converted to binary format, so this is clearly not an example for a "readable", plain text format like TXT.

I was confused by the notion in the title of why spool request would be involved at all in the file operation in ABAP. There are plenty of examples out there (including ABAP Help or very simple ones from our book) that do not involve spool. Spool has to do with printing and/or the forms. If the task was "get some data, format it nicely using a form, then download it as PDF", then yes, you'd have to deal with the spool in some way. But this is not the scenario in this post.

Second premise was "we need to automate report execution and download without user interaction". I was actually very curious how you would accomplish this. And then this post... just didn't. Do you understand what GUI_DOWNLOAD does? How would you run this "without user interaction"? Where would the file go? Try scheduling this in a background job and see what happens.

Additionally, I don't understand why would anyone need material data in a PDF format. I get it if this was a CSV or an Excel format but PDF. Why?

Here are some suggestions on how to do better next time.

  • Take a look at the SAP examples here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-cheat-sheets
  • All code examples from our ABAP book are here: https://github.com/Boneill3/IntroToABAP/tree/master They're very simple because it's a beginner book, but at least they're written using Clean ABAP and latest syntax at the time of writing.
  • I feel this file should've been an Excel spreadsheet and not PDF, so obligatory ABAP2XLSX link.
  • Always question why something is done one way and not the other. When you've completed the code, always ask yourself if there is a better way to achieve the same result.
  • Most importantly, if you say you'll write about something, then write about that. Deceiving the readers won't get you anywhere.

Good luck.

entry.title: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NET Webforms?
entry.content:
Please I need to know if Crystal Reports in ASP.NET WebForms support DoubleClickPage event to do a Drill-down feature.

I use it in my WinForms App. to open another report based on current report object info in page line.

        private void CrystalReportViewer_DoubleClickPage(object sender, PageMouseEventArgs e)
        {
            int drillbrn = 0, drilltyp = 0, drillnum = 0;
            if (e.ObjectInfos != null)
            {
                if (e.ObjectInfos.HasName("tdtbrn1"))
                {
                    foreach (ObjectInfo oi in e.ObjectInfos)
                    {
                        if (oi.Name == "tdtbrn1")
                            drillbrn = oi.Text.AsInt32();
                        if (oi.Name == "tdttyp1")
                            drilltyp = oi.Text.AsInt32();
                        if (oi.Name == "tdtnum1")
                            drillnum = oi.Text.AsInt32();
                    }
                    ReportDocument2.FileName = ReportsPath + "AccJV.rpt";
                    ReportDocument2.RecordSelectionFormula = "{AccTransHed.thd_brn} = " + drillbrn + " and {AccTransHed.thd_typ} = " + drilltyp + " and {AccTransHed.thd_num} = " + drillnum;
                    ReportDocument2.Execute(showAsDialog: true); // open another report dialog
                    ReportDocument2.Close();
                }
            }
        }

I try to ask ChatGPT for any resources. but don't find anything.

 https://chatgpt.com/share/67fd36b2-eb7c-8010-9518-f4f947b1dc0d

 

Can you please tell me how to do DoublePageClick event or alternate in ASP.NET WebForms CrystalReportVIewer.

entry.title: Automated Support Insights report generation in Customer Insights dashboard
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce the upcoming release of a new, game-changing feature in our Customer Insights Dashboard - the Automated Support Insight Report (SIR).

This automated PDF report will be generated as a self-service, offering a comprehensive and detailed view of the situation of SAP solutions from various critical perspectives such as the product portfolio, usage and adoption, cases overview, cloud availability, delivered services, and more.

There are numerous advantages and benefits of this cutting-edge feature:

  1. Holistic View: The automated SIR provides a comprehensive overview, allowing you to gain insights into the performance and utilization of SAP solutions from a single, intuitive report across various aspects.

  2. Actionable Insights & Data-Driven Decision: With the automated report generation, the identifying areas for improvement and optimization becomes very quick, enabling you to take targeted actions across the product portfolio, usage, cases, services, and other critical metrics. Equipped with detailed and relevant insights, you can make informed business decisions that drive greater operational efficiency, improving your overall SAP solution performance.

  3. Simplification and Time-saving: The automated SIR reduces the need for manual report generation, saving your team valuable time and effort, allowing you to focus on leveraging insights rather than compiling data. The report generation as an automated self-service streamlines the process of staying informed without the need for labour-intensive manual data collection and analysis.

  4. Moment Comparison: The pdf-based report allows you to "freeze the moment" and compare data from the last report generation with the next one. This enables you to track changes and performance trends over time, providing a static reference point for analysis that is not affected by real-time dashboard updates.

 You can start the Support Insight report by going to the menu “PDF Report Generator” in the upper right corner and selecting the link Download Support Insights Report.

The customer accounts you use in the dashboard will be automatically pre-selected in the default PDF-report filter. The default period is 6 months, which can be changed by using the drop-down menu.

The Support Insights report provides a various number of important KPIs across Collaboration, Innovation & Value Realization, Empowerment and Mission Critical Support.

Try it out, get your individual Support Insights report within seconds and benefit from this new automated reporting self-service.

The created report will be stored in your local download, ready to be processed as you need. 

We are confident that the automated Support Insight Report will be a valuable addition and a game-changer for your experience with SAP Customer Insights. This feature aims to provide you with actionable intelligence to enhance your SAP solution performance, enabling you to drive your business forward proactively.

Stay tuned for the rollout of this exciting new feature, and get ready to unlock the full potential of your SAP solutions with Customer Insights!

entry.title: Re: Remapping of GL expense cost element - DIP Profile/Ev...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Great question - and it’s an important one when working with Event-Based Revenue Recognition (EBRR) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

 

Scenario Recap:

You have:

  • A G/L account currently assigned to a non-billable cost element category (e.g., 90 - Statistical or 42 - Overhead).
  • The requirement is to reclassify this G/L to a billable cost element (e.g., 11 - Direct Cost).

The G/L has already been used in postings historically.

 

Best Practice in SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

You should NOT directly change the cost element category of an existing G/L account that already has posted data.

SAP explicitly recommends against this because:

  • It could corrupt revenue recognition logic.
  • It can break event-based postings, where past postings were created under one category and logic expects consistency.
  • It may lead to inconsistencies in CO or Universal Journal (ACDOCA) reporting and allocations.

 

Recommended Best Practice Steps:

1. Create a New G/L Account:

  • Copy the existing G/L using “Manage G/L Account Master Data” app.
  • Assign the correct Cost Element Category — use billable (e.g., 11 for Direct Costs).
  • Use the same account group if necessary, but make sure the account number is distinct.

2. Reassign Mappings / Derivations:

  • Update all the relevant mappings:
    • Account Determination for Revenue Recognition (SSCUI 102638)
    • Event-Based Recognition Account Assignment
    • Any relevant valuation rules, conditions, pricing procedures

3. Update Master Data / Projects:

  • Update project templates, project profiles, WBS master data, or cost objects that reference the old G/L.

4. Stop Usage of Old G/L:

  • Set the old G/L to “blocked for posting” for future use (optional, or just restrict via roles if still needed for legacy reporting).

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: How to change key in extended standard OData service?
entry.content:
I have a requirement where we need to show the Sales order line item and the Sales Order header item in one app. The Track Sales Order app (F2577) meets most of the requirement however, the business also wants to see the line items in the first page. I've been trying to work out a POC and stumbled on a small issue. Our backend developer extended the standard OData service and added 2 new fields (material # and sales document item) as well as some logic to fetch data. We tested out the service in the gateway client and the data shows up fine. The issue happens when I try to utilize the OData service in a Fiori app. I tried 3 methods:

  1. Adaptation project: I replaced the standard OData service with new OData service. However, when the data is displayed on the table, the salesDocumentItem column gets duplicated with the last item from the get request of a sales document. For example: if the get request fetches the first 20 record of a sales document, the salesDocumentItem column shows the same salesDocumentItem number which happens to be the last item from the get request 
  2. SAP Fiori elements app: I also tried to create a new Fiori elements app and utilize the service, but the same issue happens
  3. Custom Fiori app: I developed a fully custom app but instead of a smart table I used a regular sap.m responsive table with a JSON Model. The issue was solved here, and I am able to see unique salesDocumentItems in its respective column. 

After implementing the JSONModel in the custom Fiori app I realized the issue could be with the key that is implemented in the backend OData service. I noticed in SEGW that SalesDocument was the only key maintained. Ideally, we would need salesdocumentitem to be a key as well since each record is unique to the SalesDocument and salesdocumentitem. When I clicked on edit mode in SEGW and tried to click on "Is Key" for salesdocumentitem, it was blanked out and I actually could not edit any of the other properties. 

My question is, is there a way to change, add, or create a composite key for a standard OData service that has been extended?

entry.title: Team Absence Calendar - Seeing Other Managers' Direct Reports
entry.content:
Hi all, I was wondering if any other SF customers had a solution to a scenario I'm looking at regarding Time Off, specifically providing an easy way to provide multiple managers with a view of team absence outside of their direct reporting line. For example, a call centre where there may be five managers each with ten direct reports. Manager A can see their own direct reports on the Team Absence Calendar, Manager B can see their own direct reports and so on. Ideally we'd like a way that Manager B can see the Manager A's direct reports' time off, and vice-versa. 

Currently I'm thinking we can look at RBP to allow the 'unofficial' manager to view the team's absence info, but I expect this would mean they would have to go onto individual people's profile to view their team, rather than providing a way off seeing it all together in one place like with the Team Absence Calendar. The other option is by providing regular reports. 

Has anyone come across anything similar and have any knowledge to share? Any advice welcome!

entry.title: Re: Mastering SPPD/SPAU/SPAU_ENH: Your Ultimate Guide to SAP Optimization
entry.content:
It is unfortunate that latest SAP Community migration made searching for the existing blog posts nearly impossible. (Previously blogs had their own domain and it was very easy to search in Google.)

It's nice of you to share and I'm guessing because of the above issue people might be struggling to find the posts already available (other than newish SPAU_ENH, nothing changed here in decades).

It would be very valuable if you shared some personal experience and practical tips based on actual use. Otherwise instead of ultimate guide this, unfortunately, looks more like redundant information + what can be found in Help. For example, it starts with explaining "types of changes". But these transactions have nothing to do with customizing, they are for modifications. And the main reason they're used isn't actually the customers wanting to "streamline", but the old user exits like MV45AFZZ and SAP Note applications.

"Based on the project guidelines, adopt the changes" isn't very useful advise and odd repetitive number 1 makes the text difficult to read. At this point I'm not sure how trustworthy information actually is. And this is really not a "guide". This just describes the options and fields without providing much of actual guidance or explanation. If this was a city guide, it would sound like "well, there is a museum you can visit". ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

For reference, here are just some of the existing posts on this subject:
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/spdd-spau-tr-adjustments-in-spam-saint-and-sum-tool/ba-p/13499169

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/spdd-and-spau-phases-during-s-4-hana-migration/ba-p/13357796

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-discussions/tips-on-spau-spdd-from-2013-updated-to-2024/m-p/13679767/highlight/true

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/understanding-the-need-of-spdd-spau-spau-enh-in-sap-upgrade-project-in/ba-p/13485764

There is also a lot of information scattered in Q&A.

entry.title: How to create API for freight order creation using delivery document?
entry.content:
As per business requirement, we need to create freight order after delivery done. I have checked API(API_FREIGHTORDER -Freight Order (A2X) ) for Freight order creation but I am not able find correct API name for create Freight order using delivery document. Can you please provide correct API for creation of freight order using delivery document if available in S4 public cloud?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create API for freight order creation using de...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can create a freight order using delivery items through the API_FREIGHTORDER service. The specific API you are looking for is to create a freight order for delivery, and this operation is facilitated by the ‘Create for Delivery’ endpoint.

API for Creating Freight Order for Delivery: APIs for Delivery and Transportation - Create Freight Order for Delivery

To create a freight order for delivery items, you use the POST method on the FreightOrder entity. Below is a sample request that shows how you can create a freight order for delivery using the delivery document details provided.

 

API Request Example:

POST <host>/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_freightorder/FreightOrder/SAP__self.CreateForDelivery 
X-CSRF-Token: abc 
Content-Type: application/json 
Accept: application/json 
{
  '_FreightBookingItems': [ 
    {
      'FreightBookingItemUUID': 'your-uuid-here' 
    }
  ],
  'TransportationOrderType': 'your-transportation-order-type' 
}

The request requires you to specify:

  • FreightBookingItemUUID: The UUID of the freight booking item(s) linked to your delivery.
  • TransportationOrderType: The type of transportation order for the delivery you wish to create.

Ensure that the delivery items are indeed part of an existing freight booking, as the API operation depends on this linkage.

Feel free to replace 'your-uuid-here' and 'your-transportation-order-type' with actual values relevant to your system’s operation.

 

Using this API, you can programmatically create freight orders based on delivery documents, integrating seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA Cloud’s Transportation Management functionalities.

 

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Issue: Adaptation Project with Fiori Elements Flexible Programming Model (FPM)
entry.content:
Hi,

I've created an adaptation project for a Fiori Elements-based standard application: F5039 (Manage Supplier Confirmations).

Using the building blocks of the flexible programming model is straightforward when generating a custom page Fiori Elements application or using one of the provided floorplans through the generator. The building blocks just work—you don't need to configure anything, which is great!

However, I couldn't find any information on using these building blocks in an adaptation project. In my mind, they should work right out of the box, as adaptation projects are essentially apps that extend the core functionality of a standard FE application by sitting on top of it—but this doesn't seem to be the case.

Here’s the issue I’m facing:

Fragment definition (webapp/changes/fragments/ConfirmationLines.fragment.xml):

<core:FragmentDefinition
    xmlns:uxap="sap.uxap"
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:macros="sap.fe.macros">
    <uxap:ObjectPageSection
        id="op-section-257492c6"
        title="Confirmation Lines">
        <uxap:ObjectPageSubSection id="op-subsection-confirmationLines">
        <macros:Table
            id="confirmationLinesTable"
            variantManagement="Control"
            contextPath="/ConfirmationLine"
            isSearchable="false"
            metaPath="@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.LineItem"
            busy="{ui>/isBusy}"
            selectionMode="Multi"
            />  
        </uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>
    </uxap:ObjectPageSection>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

This is what the browser returns when attempting to access the view:

"Reason: Required control 'sap/fe/macros/Table' couldn't be found"

So, the FPM library isn't being loaded for some reason. I’ve tried several approaches to get this working, all without success. For example:

  • Adding a changeType: appdescr_ui5_addLibraries property to the manifest.appdescr_variant file
  • Manually requiring the modules via controller and id_* change files
  • Many others...

It seems like the "sap.fe.core.fpm" namespace is missing. In standalone Fiori Elements applications, this is typically defined in the manifest.json under the routing section, under each target definition.

UI5 version in use: 1.120.0 — though the same issue occurs even with the latest versions (e.g. 1.134.1).

Would appreciate quick assistance with this!

Thanks,

Jere

entry.title: Implementing Effective Audit Controls in SAP(Part1- Basis)
entry.content:
Implementing Effective Audit Controls in SAP  (Part1- Basis)

Audit compliance in SAP applications is not just about meeting regulatory requirements; it's about building a robust, secure, and efficient system that supports the organization's overall goals and objectives

There will be a series of audit, compliance and best practices headway for customers to consider, implement and strengthen the SAP ecosystem.

This is the first blog on SAP technical controls, next on Authorization and last on Cloud audit controls with automation insights.

Let’s get started!!  

Why We Need Audit Compliance in SAP Applications
Trust and Credibility:
1. Stakeholder Confidence: Compliance with audit standards builds trust among stakeholders, including customers, investors, and partners, by demonstrating that the organization is well-managed and reliable.
2. Reputation Management: Maintaining audit compliance helps protect and enhance the organization's reputation by showing commitment to ethical practices and transparency.

Legal and Regulatory Requirements:
1. Avoiding Penalties: Non-compliance can lead to significant fines and legal consequences. Regular audits ensure that the organization stays compliant with relevant laws and regulations.
2. Preparedness for External Audits: Internal audit compliance prepares the organization for external audits, making the process smoother and less disruptive.

Operational Excellence:
1. Continuous Improvement: Audit compliance encourages a culture of continuous improvement, where processes are regularly reviewed and optimized.
2. Risk Mitigation: Identifying and addressing risks proactively through audits helps in maintaining operational stability and resilience.

Financial Health:
1. Accurate Financial Management: Ensuring that financial records are accurate and compliant helps in better financial planning and management.
2. Investor Confidence: Reliable financial reporting and compliance attract and retain investors by showcasing the organization's financial health and stability

Illustrating SAP Basis and Security audit controls through a whitepaper, beneficial for life sciences and healthcare customers, as well as other industries.

Encourage readers to adopt best practices and utilize available resources to enhance their SAP system security.

Platform Level Access Control:

OS-Level Access Control

  • User Authentication: Implement OS authentication process or workflow to ensure that only authorized users can access the system on-premises or hyperscale's.
  • User Accounts: Create and manage user accounts with appropriate permissions using utility like useradd and passwd on UNIX/Linux or Computer Management on Windows.
  • Access Control Lists (ACLs): Set file and directory permissions to restrict access to sensitive files. Use commands like chmod, chown, and chgrp on UNIX/Linux systems.
  • Group Policies: Implement group policies on platform to manage user permissions and access rights.

DB-Level Access Control

  • User Management: Create database user accounts with specific roles and privileges. For example, you can define user attributes such as authentication method, default tablespace, and user profile
  • Roles and Privileges: Assign roles and privileges to control the types of SQL statements users can execute and the data they can access
  • Auditing and Monitoring: Monitor and record user actions on the database, including those performed by administrators. This helps in detecting and preventing unauthorized access.
  • Security Policies: Establish and enforce security policies to protect the database from accidental or malicious destruction of data. Password policies implement.

Regular Reviews:

  • Conduct Monthly / quarter reviews of user access to both the OS and DB to ensure that permissions are up-to-date and appropriate. Perform user comparison on servers.
  • All new users should have associated requests in incident management and approval.
  • Users offboarded from Company or project, personnel access removed from platform.
  • Regular review the access or modification of DB user’s privilege.
  • Annual reset the system/service user passwords to stay compliance. 

User access to third-party e.g Batch scheduler

Identify Users and Access Levels

  • List Users: Compile a list of all users who have access to the third-party applications.
  • Access Levels: Document the access levels and permissions assigned to each user.
  • Current Roles: Verify that the access levels are appropriate for the users' current roles and responsibilities.

Regular Reviews:

  • Scheduled Reviews: Conduct regular access reviews, such as quarterly or bi-annually, to ensure that access permissions are up-to-date.
  • De-provisioning: Remove access for users who no longer need it, such as those who have changed roles or left the organization

Secure Database Connections:

Securing database connections in SAP is crucial for protecting sensitive data and ensuring compliance with security standards.

Ensure Secure Network Communications

  • Use Firewall to secure inbound and outbound communications.
  • Configure SSL/TLS to secure the connection between the SAP system and the database. This involves setting up certificates and enabling SSL/TLS in the database configuration. 

Implement Strong Authentication Mechanisms

  • Role-Based Access Control (RBAC): Implement RBAC to ensure that users have the minimum necessary permissions to perform their tasks.

Enable Connectivity Logging:

  • Enable logging on oracle or HANA database to generate incoming Server name / IPs and connection string visible connection users.

 

on HANA

Transaction Code HDBSQL: Use HDBSQL to enable and configure trace settings for the HANA database.

Trace Levels: Set appropriate trace levels to capture detailed information about incoming connections.

Elevated access and user switch to <sid>adm and <DB>user 

  • Ensuring a strict process for root and administrator access is crucial for maintaining security and compliance.
  • Root access can only be granted traversing approval workflow from designated authority.
  • Elevated access should be temporary access or time-bound access.
  • Platform should have a logging enables to record root, admin, user sessions for commands getting executed.

Regular Reviews:

  • Enable and regularly review connectivity logs to validate the connections and detect any unauthorized activities.
  • All user switch to SAP technical users should be validated for commands and transactions e.g ALTER/DELETE to database tables.

Client opening: 

Enable Change Logging

  • Transaction Code SCC4: Use this transaction to configure client settings and enable change logging for client-specific activities.
  • Profile Parameters: Ensure that profile parameters like rsau/enable and rsau/selection_slots are correctly configured to capture relevant events.
  • Maintain detailed documentation of all client opening activities, including approvals and justifications
  • Access Controls: Implement strict access controls to ensure that only authorized personnel with emergency access (firefighter) can open or modify clients with change management.

Regular reviews:

  • Perform monthly review on client open change logs.
  • Match the documented changes and Firefighter access.
  • Review the SM20 security audit logs for the tasks performed with client opening.

Business Batch Jobs:

Controlling batch job modifications and failures is essential for maintaining operational efficiency and ensuring compliance during audits.

Implement Change Control Procedures

  • Approval Workflow: Establish a formal approval workflow for any modifications to batch jobs. Ensure that changes are reviewed and approved by authorized personnel before implementation.
  • Documentation: Maintain detailed documentation of all changes, including the rationale, approval, and implementation detail.
  • Report: Generate a customize report from SAP on table TBTCO/TBTCP on batch Creation,Modification,Deletion,Suspension or if using third party, schedule report to generate same.
  • Failed batch jobs handling: Implement robust error reporting mechanisms to capture and log job failures. Ensure that errors are documented and analyzed for root cause.

Ideas:

1- you can give priority to jobs based on business criticality.

2- Inimitable job naming conventions for each modules include priority and frequency.

3- Set the code-red process for high priority jobs.

4- Automate or integrate Batch failures with incident management to route responsible team.

Regular Reviews:

  1. Perform half yearly / annual review of batch job changes.
  2. Monthly review the failed batch jobs to be in control on root cause and preventive measures

SAP Parameters

  • Auditing control of SAP parameters is essential for ensuring system security and compliance.
  • Enable Security Audit Log: Use this transaction to configure the Security Audit Log. Set up filters to capture relevant events, including changes to SAP parameter
  • PAHI Table: Any changes to parameter should be visible in PAHI table and reviewed frequently.
  • Documentation: Maintain detailed documentation of all changes, including the rationale, approval, and implementation detail.

Regular Reviews:

  • PAHi table to be reviewed and vet with changes implemented along with approval.
  • User session logs on platform to be review for any profile changes on production.
  • As much as possible all profile to be arranged in alphabetical order and changes to perform from RZ10/RZ11 SAP (avoid changes from platform).

Change Management:

Auditing and controlling change management in SAP is crucial for ensuring system integrity, minimizing risks, and maintaining compliance

  • Formal Change Requests: Use a standardized process for submitting, reviewing, and approving change requests. Ensure that all changes are documented and tracked in CAB (Change authority board)
  • Approval Workflow: Establish a formal approval workflow involving multiple stakeholders to review and approve change. e.g.. Use SAP Solution Manager's ChaRM to manage and track changes across the SAP landscape. ChaRM provides tools for planning, documenting, and approving changes.
  • Quality Gate Management: Implement Quality Gate Management to ensure that changes are thoroughly tested and validated before moving to production.
  • Access Controls: Implement strict access controls to ensure that only authorized personnel can promote changes to production.
  • Documentation: Maintain detailed documentation of all changes, including approvals and justifications.
  • Access Control: STMS restricted for business and IT users. SAP Basis should only allow imports to perform. To some customers where ChARM implemented, SAP Basis only allows to import from Charm in production and STMS limited for display.

Regular Reviews:

  • Conduct regular audits to ensure that changes are implemented according to the approved process and comply with organizational policies.
  • Transport has been imported in production with authorized personnel.
  • DBTABLOG: Review of DBTABLOG which holds log changes made to database tables. This table is useful for auditing and tracking modifications to ensure data integrity and compliance.
  • CDHDR : Review of change document header table, storing information about changes made to business objects

Thanks for the read. Next blog on Authorization Control to publish shortly. 

Cheers, 

VS

entry.title: Error Connecting Power BI to Datasphere with HANA ODBC Driver
entry.content:
Hi, we facing an issue connecting Power BI to Datasphere with HANA ODBC Driver. We were able to establish the PBI to Datasphere ODBC connection through local desktop/machine but was not able to have a successful connection through PBI gateway.  The error that we are facing is "[SAP AG][LIBODBCHDB SO][HDBODBC] Communication link failure;-10709 Connection failed (RTE:[89013].Socket closed by peer". (refer to attachment for screenshot)

We went through the below notes and maintained the PBI public and local IP addresses but still not able to establish the connection:

  • 3531754 - Error Connecting Power BI to Datasphere tenant with HANA ODBC Driver
  • 3036312 - ODBC connection test to SAP Datasphere drops error Communication link failure; -10709 connection failed 

What other areas should we be checking our configurations apart from maintaining PBI's IP addresses in Datasphere's IP Allowlist. Is there a way we can trace the security logs within SAP Datasphere if there were attempts of connect from external systems?

entry.title: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providing parameter
entry.content:
Hello,

For a bit of context, I'm using ASE 16.0 SP03, an up-to-date copy of libsybdrvodb.so, Python 3.8.10, and an up-to-date copy of pyodbc ( https://github.com/mkleehammer/pyodbc )

I found a strange issue when running a simple "select" query through using pyodbc's "?" syntax. When I run this query directly in a sqsh console, it executes *very* quickly:

select * from files where filename='pgb.197901.bz2';

Adaptive Server cpu time: 0 ms. Adaptive Server elapsed time: 0 ms.

However, when I run that exact same query in a python script, it takes over a minute! The exact syntax I'm using is this:

cursor.execute("select * from files where filename=?", ['pgb.197901.bz2'])
print(cursor.fetchall())
 
I could've sworn that this wasn't an issue two months ago, which has me quite confused!
 
The above query takes over a minute, but this syntax takes just 18 ms:
cursor.execute("select * from files where filename='pgb.197901.bz2'", [])
print(cursor.fetchall())
This does work, but it's not a great solution--we want to use pyodbc's placeholder "?" syntax, because it helps to prevent SQL injection attacks.
 

I have found one other workaround which doesn't work perfectly here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/ase-15-5-poor-performance-from-odbc/qaq-p/12120748 , which suggests adding "DYNAMICPREPARE=0;" to my connection string. That indeed speeds up performance! When I use this connection string:

"DRIVER={};SERVER={};UID={};PWD={};PORT=5000;DYNAMICPREPARE=1;APP=SelectTest;"
My "cursor.execute("select * from files where filename=?", ['pgb.197901.bz2'])" query takes just 20 milliseconds (including the time taken to create the connection).
The problem with this approach is that it appears to break stored procedures! When I run this:
cursor.execute("exec get_files @filename=?", ['pgb.197901.bz2'])
print(cursor.fetchall())
I receive this error message:
"[42000] [SAP][ASE ODBC Driver][Adaptive Server Enterprise]Incorrect syntax near '?'.\n (102) (SQLExecDirectW)"
So that solution doesn't work very well for me either
 
 
To summarize, it seems like I have three bad options:
  1. Keep everything the same, but swap all my queries to only use stored procedures (not great because we want to be able to test queries out on-the-fly)
  2. Break all stored procedures, but speed up our other queries (by adding DYNAMICPREPARE). Would require large changes to our code base, so this isn't viable
  3. Make ourselves vulnerable to sql injection (insert params directly into the query string)

I'm not sure what to do--am I missing something here? Is there some way to speed up our queries so that they don't run orders-of-magnitude more slowly that *doesn't* break our existing stored procedures? Am I doing something strange in my connection information? Is there some argument that I'm missing out on?

Thank you for your help!

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP RAP Query String parameters not available to...
entry.content:
After doing much research and going through many more available blogs and SAP Help portal, I have to come to a conclusion it's not possible to pass parameter as Query String and access them in backend code. Example code on SAP Book also does not use "with Parameter" option in CDS View.

All parameters which needs to be accessed in backend should go with in Brackets (param1=10, param2=10) and all of these must be key fields.

Solution to this is create V2 service which allow Query String parameter and we can also access then easily in backend extension method of Service. Below is code for reference:

LOOP AT MR_REQUEST_DETAILS->T_URI_QUERY_PARAMETER INTO DATA(tab_parameters_line).
    WRITE: tab_parameters_line-NAME, tab_parameters_line-VALUE.
ENDLOOP.

I am still looking for solution to this requirement using V4 service.

Thanks-

entry.title: How to manage expired Pre-requistes in Success Factors LMS
entry.content:
I have a kind of a strange use case scenario, and I'm not sure if what I'm asking is even possible? (perhaps I'm just a bear of very little brains, which is fully possible)

Here's the intended use:
User takes Item A (in a curriculum to allow for re-certification every 3 years) which is a pre-requisite for Items B, C, and D (also in curriculums to allow for re-certification every 3 years). 
Items B, C and D are not only dependent on Item's A completion for assignment but also for on-going certificate validity.  So if User's certification of Item A expires, or is otherwise revoked for any reason, items B, C and D (or any combination thereof) also become invalid until such a time as the User re-certifies/becomes compliant with Item A.

Currently - the assignment profiles (of B, C and D) allow items B, C and D to be dependent on item A for assignment, but there doesn't seem to be a way to check to see if Item A is still valid down the road. So we have several users who's Item A is invalid -but their items B, C and D are still valid-even though they shouldn't be.  

What option in assignment profiles, OR some other check box somewhere am I missing? OR - is this even a possibility within SF? 

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM RFUI Outbound Picking Screen Warehouse Order...
entry.content:
Hi @SiddharthKr 

There is no place to put Warehouse Number on this screen, how are you putting it ? Warehouse Number can be accessed using parameter assigned to user.

Which Logical Transaction you are using under Picking ?

If this is the standard screen and giving dump and sure because of convertion routine, you probably need to follow below things.

1- copy standard screen and remove that conversion routine.

2- You also need to create a wrapper Function Module of PAI. Put your desired validations for these two fields. 3- Once all validation is completed call the Standard PAI Function Module at the last of your Wrapper Function Module.

4- You also need to make change in RF configuration where Instead of PAI standard Function Module now you need to call Custom Wrapper Function Module.

Hope this helps...

Thanks-

entry.title: Re: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providing parameter
entry.content:
I can't comment on pyodbc coding so just some general suggestions:

  • Have the DBA capture the query and query plan (eg, via MDA tables) to see what's being passed to ASE. (I'm guessing the search parameter is being mangled, possibly wrapped in % (wild card) characters, and ASE is opting for a table scan.)
  • With the info provided by the DBA, post a detailed question in the discussion area of that github/pyodbc page.

I'd want to verify no issues with the pyodbc code before (potentially) opening an ASE case.

entry.title: Re: Replacement of Core Tool functionalities by functionalities in Cockpit Fiori apps as of SAP CC 2
entry.content:
Hello Abhijeet, 

Thank you for giving us a heads-up about this major upcoming change. 

This shift to a cockpit looks promising and may help SAP bringing new features like AI but please also consider the performance which cockpit provides compared to core tool. Cockpit definitely gives better look and feel, help easily navigate through apps but at the end of everything it is used by business users to maintain the products and prices in tables. And if the rows count goes beyond certain limit then cockpit is not able to bear that load and that's where core tool comes in handy which seamlessly allow user to update the table.

If you can address this challenge while working on the shift then that can bring a significant improvement in user experience.

Regards,

Tanmay

entry.title: Could not commit transaction: Error -1 detected during transaction exception in ServiceLayer
entry.content:
Sap Business One Service Layer V1.26

Using a .Net OData client for Service Layer as described in the `OData Client for Service Layer` document https://help.sap.com/doc/138a52ba164b4c3197ff4d19621400a3/10.0/en-US/4e330dc617254c4e942ba42116bff90c.pdf

Since updating to Sap Business One 2502 (Could have happened prior, error is rather rare), we are getting rare, intermittent errors when using the Service Layer API to create & update activities. The activities are still modified successfully, however the response we are getting indicates some sort of failure.

Has anyone else had this issue?
Is there logs in Sap that I can check to help diagnose this issue further?

Thanks in advance 🙂

Exception & Error json

Microsoft.OData.Client.DataServiceClientException: { "error" : { "code" : "-1116", "details" : [ { "code" : "", "message" : "" } ], "message" : "Could not commit transaction: Error -1 detected during transaction" } }
